medicines.name: Paracetamol (Panadol)
medicines.description: Effective fever and pain reliever used for headache, body ache, and toothache. Safe for all age groups when taken in correct dosages.
medicines.usageInstructions: Adults: Take 1-2 tablets every 4-6 hours. Do not exceed 8 tablets in 24 hours.
medicines.warnings: Do not combine with other Paracetamol-containing medications. Avoid alcohol.

medicines.name: Amoxil (Amoxicillin)
medicines.description: Broad-spectrum penicillin antibiotic used to treat bacterial infections of the ears, throat, lungs, urinary tract, and skin.
medicines.usageInstructions: Take 1 capsule 3 times daily (every 8 hours) for 5-7 days as directed. Finish the full course.
medicines.warnings: Requires pharmacist review and prescription upload. Do not take if allergic to Penicillin.

medicines.name: Glucophage (Metformin)
medicines.description: First-line medication for the treatment of type 2 diabetes, helping lower blood glucose levels and improve insulin response.
medicines.usageInstructions: Take 1 tablet twice daily with breakfast and dinner. Swallow whole with water.
medicines.warnings: Consult doctor for renal status check. Monitor blood sugar regularly.

medicines.name: Lipitor (Atorvastatin)
medicines.description: Statin class cholesterol-lowering medication used to prevent cardiovascular disease in high-risk patients.
medicines.usageInstructions: Take 1 tablet daily at night, with or without food. Maintain a low-fat diet.
medicines.warnings: Contraindicated in pregnancy. Report any unexplained muscle pain to your pharmacist immediately.

medicines.name: Cetriz (Cetirizine)
medicines.description: Non-drowsy 24-hour antihistamine that provides rapid relief from allergic rhinitis, runny nose, watery eyes, and itching.
medicines.usageInstructions: Adults: 1 tablet daily before sleeping. Do not exceed 1 tablet per day.
medicines.warnings: Avoid driving or operating heavy machinery if you feel drowsy.

medicines.name: Seven Seas Cod Liver Oil
medicines.description: Premium pure cod liver oil capsules packed with natural Omega-3, EPA, DHA, Vitamin A, and Vitamin D to support immunity, joints, and brain development.
medicines.usageInstructions: Take 1-2 capsules daily with meals. Ideal for both elderly citizens and growing children.
medicines.warnings: Do not exceed recommended intake unless advised by a nutritionist.

medicines.name: Nestlé Cerelac (Wheat & Apple)
medicines.description: Nutritious infant cereal fortified with iron, zinc, and key vitamins to help support child growth and mental development starting from 6 months.
medicines.usageInstructions: Mix 3 level scoops of Cerelac with 150ml of warm pre-boiled drinking water until smooth. Feed immediately.
medicines.warnings: Breastfeeding is best for babies. Clean all utensils thoroughly before preparing food.

medicines.name: Ensure Gold (Vanilla)
medicines.description: Complete, balanced nutrition formula scientifically designed to build muscle mass, strength, and energy levels for older adults.
medicines.usageInstructions: Add 6 level scoops of powder into 185ml of cold or room-temperature water. Stir well.
medicines.warnings: Not intended for children unless recommended by a pediatric doctor.

medicines.name: Sensodyne Rapid Relief
medicines.description: Clinically proven toothpaste providing rapid relief and long-lasting protection for sensitive teeth and exposed dentin.
medicines.usageInstructions: Brush thoroughly twice daily, not more than 3 times, minimizing swallowing.
medicines.warnings: Not for children under 12 years of age unless recommended by a dentist.

medicines.name: Ventolin Inhaler (Salbutamol)
medicines.description: Rapid-acting bronchodilator used to relieve acute bronchospasm, asthma attacks, wheezing, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).
medicines.usageInstructions: Inhale 1 or 2 puffs as needed for shortness of breath. Maximum 8 puffs in 24 hours.
medicines.warnings: Consult with a physician if you require emergency rescue therapy more than twice weekly.

medicines.name: Advil (Ibuprofen)
medicines.description: Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) used to treat headache, joint pain, muscle strain, backache, and toothache by targeting active inflammation.
medicines.usageInstructions: Take 1 capsule every 6-8 hours with water and a light meal. Do not exceed 3 capsules in 24 hours.
medicines.warnings: Do not take if you have a history of stomach ulcers, renal impairment, or active asthma triggers.

medicines.name: Augmentin (625mg)
medicines.description: High-potency combined penicillin antibiotic for severe respiratory, ENT, skin, and soft tissue bacterial infections resistant to normal amoxicillin.
medicines.usageInstructions: Take 1 tablet twice daily exactly with or at start of meals to reduce gastric distress.
medicines.warnings: Do not take if you have a history of cholestatic jaundice or penicillin hypersensitivity.

medicines.name: Neurobion Forte
medicines.description: Synergistic vitamin blend supporting healthy peripheral nervous system, nerve fiber regeneration, and relief from diabetes-related neuropathy.
medicines.usageInstructions: Take 1 tablet daily with breakfast. Swallow whole without crushing.
medicines.warnings: Store below 25°C in a dry cabinet. Keep out of reach of children.

medicines.name: Dettol Liquid Antiseptic
medicines.description: Effective concentrated antiseptic disinfectant for safe wound cleansing, surgical skin disinfection, and clinical environmental sanitation.
medicines.usageInstructions: Dilute 1 part Dettol with 20 parts clean water before applying to cuts, scratches, or bites.
medicines.warnings: For external skin or environment use only. Do NOT swallow or apply near sensitive mucosa or ears.

medicines.name: Co-Diovan (160/12.5mg)
medicines.description: Combined angiotensin II receptor antagonist and thiazide diuretic used to manage severe hypertension and protect heart function.
medicines.usageInstructions: Take 1 tablet daily in the morning with a full glass of water. Keep dosage timing stable.
medicines.warnings: Contraindicated in pregnancy due to risks of fetal toxicity. Monitor blood pressure weekly.

blogs.title: Managing Type-2 Diabetes Under Warm Climates
blogs.content: With rising temperatures in Jaffna, chronic patients, particularly diabetes patients taking Metformin, must stay hydrated. Dehydration leads to poor renal flow, increasing metformin side effects. Maintain blood sugar tests twice weekly, drink plenty of herbal drinks without sugar (such as neem tea or water), and keep medications below 25°C inside a dark shelf.
blogs.author: Dr. K. Gnanapragasam

blogs.title: Seasonal Dengue Outbreaks: Safe OTC Fever Care
blogs.content: During monsoonal seasons, dengue vector mosquitoes thrive. If you develop a sudden fever, standard Paracetamol is the ONLY safe self-treatment. NEVER consume NSAIDs like Ibuprofen, Diclofenac, or Aspirin, as they drastically increase the risk of internal bleeding if the fever is Dengue Hemorrhagic. If fever lasts over 48 hours, proceed immediately to the Jaffna Teaching Hospital for a full blood count.
blogs.author: Ms. Ahilya Selvam

blogs.title: Healthy Nutrition Tips for Golden Elderly Years
blogs.content: Muscular atrophy (sarcopenia) and poor joint fluid are common complaints among senior citizens. Fortifying their breakfast with adult milk formulas containing CaHMB and Vitamin D is proven to maintain skeletal and muscle health. Ensure light walks in the morning sun to trigger natural Vitamin D, and combine with low-sodium diets.
blogs.author: Dr. Thivya Aruliah

interactions.description: No clinical interaction observed. Safe to take concurrently with appropriate timing.
Safe and frequently prescribed together for type 2 diabetes with lipid control. Ensure kidney function tests are stable.
No mutual side effects. Avoid alcohol ingestion during active pain/fever therapy.
Amoxicillin may reduce systemic absorption and efficacy of oral contraceptive pills. Use secondary barrier methods during antibiotic therapy.
Red level alert! High risk of profound systemic bleeding. Combined use impairs platelet aggregation and coagulation simultaneously.
Clarithromycin dramatically increases statin concentrations, heightening risk of acute muscle breakdown (rhabdomyolysis). Hold statin during antibiotic course.

ocrDetails.dosage: Take 1 capsule 3 times daily (every 8 hours) with water
ocrDetails.extractedText: Rx: Amoxicillin 500mg capsules. Dispense XXI (21 capsules). Sig: i cap t.i.d. pc. For bacterial chest infection. Dr. S. Kirupakaran, Teaching Hospital Jaffna.

appointments.notes: Consultation regarding diabetic medication side effects.
